fields.label: Account Name
fields.description: Your LogicMonitor account name. Example: Use foo for the logic monitor URL https://foo.logicmonitor.com/`

fields.label: API v3 Key
fields.description: Bearer token for LogicMonitor REST API v3.

fields.label: Token
fields.description: Token for accessing the datasource API

groups.title: Connection
groups.description: Provide information to connect to the data source

instructions.msg: LogicMonitor configuration is stored under a service-keyed jsonData shape: jsonData.services.logicmonitor.* holds per-service settings and jsonData.variables.<name> holds connection variables, while secrets use flat dotted secureJsonData keys of the form "logicmonitor.<secret>". There is a single service with id 'logicmonitor'.

Authentication uses a LogicMonitor REST API v3 bearer token. jsonData.services.logicmonitor.auth.id is 'auth_bearer' (the backend defaults to it) and the token is the write-only secret at secureJsonData key "logicmonitor.token".

Connection: the server URL is built from the 'account_name' variable as https://{account_name}.logicmonitor.com/santaba/rest. Set jsonData.variables.account_name to the account subdomain only (e.g. 'foo' for https://foo.logicmonitor.com/). The single server is fixed, so no server selector is shown.

The secret at secureJsonData key "logicmonitor.token" is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether it is already configured, read secureJsonFields["logicmonitor.token"] == true rather than secureJsonData.
